title: Columbaria vulgaris
intro: Columbaria vulgaris, also known as rockjasmine or rock jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Columbaria vulgaris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Columbaria vulgaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Columbaria vulgaris is a type of flowering plant, but I couldn't find any information on its classification. parts: Columbaria vulgaris, also known as the Wild Pansy, is a plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. side_effects: Columbaria vulgaris is a type of flowering plant, and like many other plants, it's not typically associated with side effects in humans. However, if ingested, it may cause gastrointestinal issues due to its toxicity. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to other adverse effects, including liver and kidney damage. It's essential to note that the plant is not typically used for medicinal purposes in humans. If you suspect exposure to Columbaria vulgaris, it's crucial to seek medical attention immediately.
